events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a shaft alignment task at the "rough-complete" stage. The observed field condition is "soft foot uncorrected" with a dial reading visible in a photo (though I can't see the photo, the text says "ME" which likely refers to a measurement). Soft foot is a condition where a machine's feet don't sit flat on the baseplate, causing distortion when bolts are tightened, which affects alignment.

Soft foot must be corrected before performing shaft alignment. API 686 requires soft foot to be checked and corrected to within specified tolerances (typically 0.002" or 0.05mm) before alignment proceeds. Uncorrected soft foot is a defect that will compromise alignment quality.

Since the soft foot is uncorrected, this is a fail. The component condition is "installed-defective" since the installation has a defect (uncorrected soft foot).

Let me construct the JSON response.

    "Soft foot must be corrected before final alignment; leaving it uncorrected will induce frame distortion when hold-down bolts are torqued, leading to misalignment, bearing wear, and premature coupling failure"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Loosen hold-down bolts sequentially and use feeler gauges or a dial indicator to identify which feet are soft",
    "Install appropriate stainless steel shims under the soft foot to bring deflection within API 686 soft-foot tolerance (typically ≤0.002 in / 0.05 mm)",
    "Re-verify soft foot at all feet after shimming, then proceed with laser or dial-indicator shaft alignment"
  ],
  "rationale": "API 686 requires soft foot to be checked and corrected to within specified limits before shaft alignment is performed. An uncorrected soft foot distorts the machine frame when bolts are tightened, making any alignment readings invalid and risking vibration, bearing overload, and coupling damage. At rough-complete stage this defect must be resolved before the work can proceed to final alignment and closeout.",
